dialogues: All of these show bags with items inside. But can I write a multiplication expression for all of them? Remember, multiplication shows EQUAL groups. Select the pictures where every bag has the SAME number inside.

You got it. The first picture has 3 bags with 4 in each. Equal groups, 3 times 4. The third picture has 4 bags with 3 in each. Equal groups, 4 times 3. The other two? Different amounts in the bags so they have unequal groups. We can't write a multiplication expression for those.
